cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the context. The passage says that Babbage continued his work in London for four years after winning the prize in 1822. Then, his health broke down, and he took a long holiday abroad. When he returned in 1828, he was at the end of his resources, many bills were unpaid, his assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with expensive tools. For one year no work was done.
